tasks.label: Go out to the club with the friend group

memory.summary: My friends are pushing hard for me to join them tonight, but my midterm tomorrow demands those final couple of hours I need. I'll call it a day now; I can't risk failing just because I'm feeling pressured.
I finally stood my ground with Mina and Jae, prioritizing my midterm over their plan. It feels right to protect that future payoff, even if it strains the moment.
I held my ground against the group, but now that the club night is gone and I've studied hard, I feel a mix of relief and regret for missing our connection.

Resting fully restores my energy, yet the regret of missing tonight's connection lingers. I must find a way to honor both my long-term goals and my relationships without sacrificing one for the other.
With the midterm secured, I can finally shift from defense to connection. My energy is restored, so I'm ready to make amends with Mina and Jae before it's too late.

I cleared the exam prep and messaged Mina, but I realize my delay in reconnecting after the club night has strained our trust. Now that the midterm begins, I must immediately repair this bond before focusing on new tasks.
Sending the message to Mina now that the exam is clear feels like a necessary step, but I still dread how my previous silence might have hurt our bond.
My message to Mina landed, but I fear my prior silence still weighs on her. Restoring this bond feels more urgent than any new task.

desires: Repair the trust fracture with Mina immediately before her perception of my reliability erodes further
Reintegrate into the cohort's social rhythm (club night) to avoid the isolation that fuels my anxiety
Ensure I don't slip back into the cycle of prioritizing grades over connection when the midterm pressure rises again

goals.description: Immediately follow up with Mina and Jae in the group chat to explicitly frame my refusal as an act of valuing our relationship enough to be honest, not a rejection, while offering concrete alternative ways to connect that don't interfere with exam prep.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value (0.5) is my highest driver; leaving things vague risks permanent damage to cohort_3. My assertiveness (8) requires me to clarify the 'why' now rather than letting silence create false narratives. I must renegotiate this social contract immediately to protect the long-term bond, even if it feels uncomfortable.

goals.description: Execute strict sleep discipline: lock down screens and rest immediately after finishing the follow-up message, resisting the urge to over-explain or seek further validation before bed.
goals.rationale: Despite the emotional cost of setting boundaries, my health priority and high sleep discipline (8) dictate that sacrificing sleep today guarantees failure tomorrow. Protecting my energy reserves is the only way to maintain the reliability required for both my academic goals and my future interactions with friends.

agent.last_reasoning: The message to Mina is sent; the social contract is renegotiated. Now I must honor my sleep discipline (8) to protect long-term reliability. Staying up risks burnout, which would ultimately hurt both my grades and future friendship quality.

goals.description: Draft and send a firm, guilt-free refusal to Mina and Jae in the group chat, explicitly stating that academic commitment is non-negotiable right now.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value prioritizes my close friend group (cohort_3), but my assertiveness axis demands I set boundaries directly rather than passively. Procrastinating on this creates unnecessary guilt which drains my focus for tomorrow's exam. I need to renegotiate this boundary *now* to preserve the relationship long-term by showing them I respect my own commitments.

goals.description: Complete the remaining 40% of midterm review immediately before bed to ensure I am reliable and prepared for the 9am exam.
goals.rationale: With sleep debt already at 2 hours and a hard deadline tomorrow morning, skipping study now would jeopardize my performance. My deliberateness requires investing time now for future payoff; being exhausted tomorrow makes me unreliable to both the professor and myself, which conflicts with my values.
